title: States Weekly: June 24, 2026
text:
States Weekly: June 24, 2026

Newsletter
by

Aryan D’Rozario

Published June 24, 2026

Big News: Kerala announces the Oommen Chandy Health Insurance Scheme; Delhi announces a Proactive Winter Air Quality Management Framework; Gujarat announces the Viksit Gujarat Industrial Policy 2026; Maharashtra approves the Maharashtra Automated Systems Policy 2026; Bihar announces MVR revisions alongside the Bihar Stamp (Prevention of Under-Valuation of Instruments) (Amendment) Rules, 2026; Maharashtra approves the Maharashtra Rural Drinking Water Policy 2026; and more.Health Jharkhand launches the “District Sandbox” Initiative. The initiative aims to create a real-world testing environment, enabling innovators, startups, and researchers to test medical technologies in public health settings before large-scale deployment.Source: Digital Health NewsIn detail: Press ReleaseKerala announces the Oommen Chandy Health Insurance Scheme. The scheme will provide support of up to $26,416 (INR 25 lakh) per family.Source: New Indian ExpressIn detail: Kerala Budget Speech 2026-27Climate Change and EnergyDelhi announces a Proactive Winter Air Quality Management Framework. The framework introduces a series of measures, including doubling of parking charges, restrictions on non-Bharat Stage VI goods vehicles, mandatory pollution certificates for fuel purchases and staggered office timings. The measures will remain in force for four months from November every year.Source: India TodayDelhi issues Draft DERC (Terms and Conditions for Green Energy Open Access) (First Amendment) Regulations, 2026. The regulations, issued by the Delhi Electricity Regulatory Commission (DERC), aim to make Green Energy Open Access more accessible to a larger number of electricity consumers, based on the sanctioned load or contracted demand of the consumer.Source: Solar QuarterIn detail: DERC (Terms and Conditions for Green Energy Open Access) (First Amendment) Regulations, 2026Assam announces plans to list the Assam Electricity Grid Corporation Limited (AEGCL) and Assam Power Generation Corporation Limited (AGCL) on the Stock Exchange. The decision aims to enable these corporations to access capital markets and raise resources.Source: India TodayChhattisgarh releases the CSERC Tariff Order for Financial Year 2026-27. The order, issued by the Chhattisgarh State Electricity Regulatory Commission (CSERC), approves an average electricity tariff increase of 6.23 percent across all consumer categories, effective July 1, 2026.Source: VyqonIn detail: CSERC Tariff Order for Financial Year 2026-27Himachal Pradesh releases the HPERC Electricity Grid Code Regulations, 2026. The regulations, issued by the Himachal Pradesh Electricity Regulatory Commission (HPERC), aim to strengthen the planning, operation, management, protection, and security of the state’s electricity grid while enabling deeper integration of renewable energy resources.Source: Solar QuarterIn detail: HPERC Electricity Grid Code Regulations, 2026Meghalaya releases Draft State Grid Code Regulations, 2026. The regulations, issued by the Meghalaya State Electricity Regulatory Commission (MSERC), aim to align with National Standards, and to garner feedback from the public.Source: Solar QuarterIn detail: Meghalaya State Grid Code Regulations 2026FinanceNothing critical to report this week.Industrial Policy and Business RegulationsGujarat announces the Viksit Gujarat Industrial Policy 2026. The policy sets out a framework which aims to establish Gujarat as a global leader in advanced manufacturing, innovation, and sustainable development.Source: News MillIn detail: Viksit Gujarat Industrial Policy 2026Haryana issues the Haryana Agri-Business and Food Processing Policy-2026. The policy aims to establish Haryana as a leading destination for food processing investments through modern infrastructure, cluster-based development, and stronger farm-to-market linkages.Source: Times of IndiaMaharashtra approves the Maharashtra Automated Systems Policy 2026. The policy seeks to accelerate growth across the entire automated systems ecosystem, covering research and development, innovation, manufacturing, deployment, operations, maintenance and repair.Source: SwarajyaAssam issues the Assam Excise (Amendment) Rules 2026. The new rules introduce major changes in liquor sales, licensing, location of liquor outlets, revenue collection, and the promotion of heritage alcoholic beverages. The new rules mandate minimum guaranteed revenue payments for liquor license holders, impose stricter distance norms for retail outlets, and regulate liquor sales and license transfers.Source: Deccan HeraldIn detail: Assam Excise (Amendment) Rules 2026Land Acquisition and Labor RegulationsBihar announces MVR revisions alongside the Bihar Stamp (Prevention of Under-Valuation of Instruments) (Amendment) Rules, 2026. The state announces that under the new Minimum Value Register (MVR), or “circle rates,” rural land rates will increase by 60 percent. The general Stamp Duty rate has also been increased by 1 percent. For land registered in a woman’s name, the concession on stamp duty has been increased from 0.3 percent to 0.4 percent, while the 0.1 percent concession on registration fees remains unchanged. This raises the total concession available to women to 0.5%.Source: Times of IndiaIn detail: Stamp Duty Notification, Bihar Stamp (Prevention of Under-Valuation of Instruments) (Amendment) Rules, 2026Goa announces around 2,791 acres as a No-Development Zone. The decision aims to safeguard Goa's environmentally fragile area.Source: ETVIn detail: NotificationInfrastructure and GovernanceKarnataka announces a 50% concession on traffic fines between June 21 and July 10. The order applies to the capital city of Bengaluru.Source: Indian ExpressIn detail: Karnataka Traffic Concession NotificationMaharashtra revises Maharashtra Right to Information Rules, 2026, based on the Right to Information (RTI) Act. The application fee for seeking information rises from $0.10 to $0.31 (INR 10 to 30), while the cost of obtaining copies of documents has increased from $0.02 to $0.05 (INR 2 to 5) per page.Source: Hindustan TimesIn detail: NotificationTelangana approves a 1.621 percent increase in Dearness Allowance (DA) for electricity department staff, pensioners, and artisans.Source: MintWest Bengal announces a 20 percent increase in Dearness Allowance (DA) and Dearness Relief (DR) for government employees and pensioners. The new rate will be effective from October 1, 2026.Source: MintArunachal Pradesh amends the Arunachal Pradesh Land Settlement and Records Rules (APLSR), 2012. The new rules aim to simplify land-use regulations and remove procedural hurdles concerning agricultural land, among other issues.Source: Arunachal ObserverIn detail: Press ReleaseArunachal Pradesh approves the Arunachal Pradesh Cooperation Policy 2026. The policy seeks to revitalize cooperative institutions, strengthen digital governance systems, and improve market access for the agriculture sector.Source: Arunachal ObserverIn detail: Press ReleaseKerala announces a Single-Window Invest Kerala Cell. The government aims to assist in establishing 10,000 MSMEs, and create a unified platform to handle land acquisition, regulatory approvals, and post-investment support.Source: New Indian ExpressIn detail: Kerala Budget Speech 2026-27Ladakh announces a ban on single-use plastics. Violations will include fines of up to $105 (INR 10,000).Source: NDTVIn detail: Press ReleaseWater and SanitationMaharashtra approves the Maharashtra Rural Drinking Water Policy 2026. The policy focuses on effective and permanent measures to maintain the supply of drinking water in rural areas.Source: Times of IndiaCenter – State RelationsNothing critical to report this week.Any OtherAndhra Pradesh relaxes registration rules for doctors. Doctors from across the country will be permitted to practice in the state without requiring a separate registration from the local medical council or a non-objection certificate from their home state councils.Source: RediffBihar issues Draft Bihar Outstanding Sportspersons Direct Recruitment (Amendment) Rules, 2026. The rules expand government employment opportunities for athletes who excel at national and international competitions.Source: The HinduIn detail: Cabinet Decisions
